reason: Self-improver approval and execution endpoints are currently open, which is risky for monetization readiness and production reliability. Adding optional API key protection plus a health endpoint that reports output and proposal counts makes the system safer to operate and easier to commercialize.

def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}.

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "script": script,
    }

title: Add input normalization to generation models for more consistent output quality
reason: User-entered payload fields currently pass through with inconsistent whitespace and formatting, which degrades generation consistency across products and videos. Normalizing inputs at the API boundary improves product output quality, reliability, and user flow without changing the product surface.

steps.find:
class GenerateRequest(BaseModel):
    topic: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
    niche: str = Field(default="Make Money Online")
    tone: str = Field(default="Premium")
    buyer: str = Field(default="Beginners starting from zero")
    promise: str = Field(default="")
    bonus: str = Field(default="")
    notes: str = Field(default="")
    duration_seconds: int = Field(default=35, ge=10, le=90)


class BatchRequest(GenerateRequest):
    count: int = Field(default=3, ge=1, le=10)

Tight validation and fail-fast execution will directly improve self-improver quality, reliability, and approval safety.

title: Add product quality scoring to generated YouTube packs
reason: Right now long-form output is returned as raw text without any built-in quality signal. A simple deterministic quality score with monetization and clarity checks improves output quality, helps future self-improver evaluation loops, and gives the product a stronger readiness layer for founder workflows.

steps.find:
def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}.

POSITIONING:
Primary audience: {buyer}
Core promise: {promise or f'Get better results with {topic}'}
Tone: {tone}
Suggested offer: low-ticket digital product + upsell bundle

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

HOOK OPTIONS:
- Most people are making {topic} harder than it needs to be.
- If you have been stuck trying to win with {topic}, this is the reset.
- Here is the cleaner system for {buyer.lower()} who want real momentum.

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.
Use one framework, track what works, and turn small wins into a repeatable process.
That is how beginners stop consuming and start compounding results.

CTA IDEAS:
- Download the companion execution pack.
- Grab the prompt bundle linked below.
- Use the checklist and templates to implement this faster.

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "script": script,
    }
steps.replace:
def _score_youtube_pack(title: str, script: str, promise: str, bonus: str) -> Dict:
    checks = {
        "has_strong_title": len(title.strip()) >= 20,

    return {"score": score, "checks": checks}

    items = []

            if not isinstance(items, list):

        "captured_at": __import__("time").time(),

steps.find:
def propose(title, reason, steps):
    if not isinstance(title, str) or not title.strip():
        raise ValueError("title is required")
    if not isinstance(reason, str) or len(reason.strip()) < 10:
        raise ValueError("reason must be at least 10 characters")
    _validate_steps(steps)
    title = title.strip()
    reason = reason.strip()
    d = load()
    fingerprint = _fingerprint(title, steps)

    for existing in d:
        if existing.get("fingerprint") == fingerprint and existing.get("status") in {"pending", "approved", "applied"}:
            return existing

    p = {
        "id": new_id(),
        "title": title,
        "reason": reason,
        "steps": steps,
        "status": "pending",
        "fingerprint": fingerprint,
        "created_at": int(time.time())
    }
    d.append(p)
    save(d)
    return p

def log_lead(source: str, payload: GenerateRequest, job_id: str | None = None) -> None:
    if not payload.customer_email and not payload.customer_name:
        return

    items = []
    if LEADS_FILE.exists():
        try:
            items = json.loads(LEADS_FILE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            if not isinstance(items, list):
                items = []
        except Exception:
            items = []

    items.append({
        "source": source,
        "job_id": job_id,
        "topic": payload.topic,
        "niche": payload.niche,
        "tone": payload.tone,
        "buyer": payload.buyer,
        "promise": payload.promise,
        "customer_name": payload.customer_name,
        "customer_email": str(payload.customer_email) if payload.customer_email else "",
        "marketing_consent": payload.marketing_consent,
        "created_at": int(__import__("time").time()),
    })
    temp_file = LEADS_FILE.with_suffix(".tmp")
    temp_file.write_text(json.dumps(items, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    temp_file.replace(LEADS_FILE)

@router.get("/stats")
def stats_route():
    items = load()
    executions = _load_execution_log()
    counts = {
        "pending": 0,
        "approved": 0,
        "applied": 0,
        "rejected": 0,
        "failed": 0,
    }
    risk_counts = {
        "low": 0,
        "medium": 0,
        "high": 0,
    }
    total_steps = 0
    for item in items:
        status = item.get("status")
        if status in counts:
            counts[status] += 1
        steps = item.get("steps", [])
        summary = item.get("summary") or summarize_steps(steps)
        risk = item.get("risk") or assess_proposal_risk(steps)
        total_steps += summary["step_count"]
        if risk.get("level") in risk_counts:
            risk_counts[risk["level"]] += 1

    success_count = sum(1 for item in executions if item.get("ok"))
    failure_count = sum(1 for item in executions if item.get("ok") is False)
    avg_duration_ms = round(
        sum(item.get("duration_ms", 0) for item in executions if isinstance(item.get("duration_ms"), (int, float))) / len(executions),
        2,
    ) if executions else 0

    counts["total"] = len(items)
    counts["avg_steps"] = round(total_steps / len(items), 2) if items else 0
    counts["risk_counts"] = risk_counts
    counts["execution_metrics"] = {
        "total_runs": len(executions),
        "success_count": success_count,
        "failure_count": failure_count,
        "success_rate": round(success_count / len(executions), 3) if executions else 0,
        "avg_duration_ms": avg_duration_ms,
    }
    return counts

steps.replace:
def _score_product_pack(topic: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    topic_text = (topic or "").strip()
    promise_text = (promise or "").strip().lower()
    bonus_text = (bonus or "").strip().lower()
    notes_text = (notes or "").strip().lower()

    checks = {
        "has_specific_topic": len(topic_text) >= 4,
        "has_clear_promise": bool(promise_text),
        "has_bonus": bool(bonus_text),
        "has_notes": bool(notes_text),
        "has_outcome_language": any(word in promise_text for word in ["get", "build", "launch", "grow", "improve", "start", "sell", "earn", "save"]),
        "has_specificity": any(token in promise_text for token in ["days", "weeks", "steps", "system", "checklist", "template", "framework"]),
        "has_monetization_signal": any(word in bonus_text + " " + notes_text for word in ["offer", "price", "bundle", "upsell", "order bump", "lead", "cta", "checkout"]),
        "has_execution_signal": any(word in notes_text + " " + promise_text for word in ["execute", "implementation", "action", "workflow", "launch", "plan"]),
    }
    score = int(sum(1 for passed in checks.values() if passed) / len(checks) * 100)
    verdict = "strong" if score >= 85 else "good" if score >= 65 else "needs_improvement"
    return {"score": score, "verdict": verdict, "checks": checks}

steps.content:
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Dict, List


def clean_text(value: str, fallback: str = "") -> str:
    value = (value or "").strip()
    return value if value else fallback


def title_case(value: str) -> str:
    return " ".join(word.capitalize() for word in clean_text(value).split())


def build_positioning_block(topic: str, niche: str, buyer: str, promise: str) -> Dict:
    topic_text = title_case(clean_text(topic, "Digital Product"))
    niche_text = clean_text(niche, "online business")
    buyer_text = clean_text(buyer, "beginners starting from zero")
    promise_text = clean_text(promise, f"get better results with {topic_text}")
    return {
        "angle": f"A premium but practical {topic_text} product for {buyer_text} in the {niche_text} niche.",
        "promise": promise_text,
        "audience": buyer_text,
        "why_now": f"Buyers want faster execution, less confusion, and clearer outcomes around {topic_text}.",
        "differentiation": [
            "Faster to consume than a bloated course",
            "More actionable than generic advice threads",
            "Designed to create implementation momentum quickly",
        ],
    }


def build_launch_plan(topic: str, buyer: str) -> List[str]:
    topic_text = title_case(clean_text(topic, "Digital Product"))
    buyer_text = clean_text(buyer, "beginners")
    return [
        f"Lead with a simple promise-driven post introducing the {topic_text} offer for {buyer_text}.",
        "Publish 3 short-form clips that show one quick win, one mistake, and one framework.",
        "Offer the front-end product first, then present the order bump at checkout.",
        "Follow up with a bundle angle that increases perceived value instead of adding complexity.",
        "Reuse testimonials-style outcomes and transformation bullets in every promotion asset.",
    ]


def build_cta_bank(topic: str, promise: str, bonus: str) -> List[str]:
    topic_text = title_case(clean_text(topic, "Digital Product"))
    promise_text = clean_text(promise, f"move faster with {topic_text}")
    bonus_text = clean_text(bonus, "Bonus Prompt Pack")
    return [
        f"Get the {topic_text} Execution Pack and start using a cleaner system today.",
        f"Use this pack to {promise_text} without wasting weeks on scattered advice.",
        f"Grab the front-end offer now and add {bonus_text} to implement faster.",
        f"If you want clarity, momentum, and a stronger offer path, start with the {topic_text} pack.",
        f"Download the bundle if you want the full implementation path instead of piecing it together yourself.",
    ]


def build_value_stack(topic: str, bonus: str) -> List[Dict]:
    topic_text = title_case(clean_text(topic, "Digital Product"))
    bonus_text = clean_text(bonus, "Bonus Prompt Pack")
    return [
        {"item": f"{topic_text} core guide", "value": "$19"},
        {"item": f"{topic_text} templates", "value": "$14"},
        {"item": f"{topic_text} checklist", "value": "$9"},
        {"item": bonus_text, "value": "$9"},
        {"item": "launch and CTA assets", "value": "$12"},
    ]

steps.content:
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Dict, List


def build_sales_assets(topic: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str) -> Dict:
    topic_text = (topic or "Digital Product").strip()
    buyer_text = (buyer or "Beginners").strip()
    promise_text = (promise or f"get better results with {topic_text}").strip()
    bonus_text = (bonus or "Bonus Prompt Pack").strip()

    return {
        "hero": {
            "headline": f"{topic_text.title()} Execution Pack",
            "subheadline": f"A premium shortcut for {buyer_text.lower()} who want to {promise_text}.",
            "primary_cta": "Get the execution pack",
            "secondary_cta": f"Add {bonus_text} at checkout",
        },
        "bullets": [
            f"Built for {buyer_text.lower()}, not advanced operators.",
            "Designed to reduce confusion and speed up execution.",
            "Includes practical assets you can use immediately.",
            "Structured to feel premium without adding complexity.",
        ],
        "objection_handling": [
            "I do not need more theory. I need something usable.",
            "I want a cleaner starting point, not a giant course.",
            "I need faster implementation, not more tabs open.",
        ],
        "cta_lines": [
            "Start with the pack. Add the bonus. Implement today.",
            "Get the shortcut and skip the messy trial-and-error phase.",
            "Use the pack to move from idea overload to execution.",
        ],
        "checkout_notes": [
            "Keep the front-end low-friction and outcome-led.",
            "Present the order bump as the fastest implementation enhancer.",
            "Anchor the bundle as the complete version for serious buyers.",
        ],
    }

    checks = {

        "has_promise": bool((promise or "").strip()),
        "has_bonus": bool((bonus or "").strip()),
        "has_notes": bool((notes or "").strip()),
        "has_outcome_language": any(word in (promise or "").lower() for word in ["get", "build", "launch", "grow", "improve", "start"]),
    }
    score = int(sum(1 for passed in checks.values() if passed) / len(checks) * 100)
    verdict = "strong" if score >= 80 else "good" if score >= 60 else "needs_improvement"
    return {"score": score, "verdict": verdict, "checks": checks}

6. Promote with content focused on one problem and one outcome
7. Track clicks, conversion rate, and average order value weekly

CTA:
Deliver or promote this pack here: {cta_url}
"""

    filename = f"{topic_text.replace(' ', '_')}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}.pdf"
    _make_pdf(title, content, filename)

    return {
        "title": title,
        "variant": variant_text,

def build_product_batch(**kwargs):
    count = kwargs.get("count", 3)
    base_variants = [
        "Beginner quick-start angle",
        "Premium transformation angle",
        "Fast-results action plan angle",
        "Simple framework angle",
        "Monetization-first offer angle",
        "Audience pain-point angle",
        "Creator productivity angle",
        "Low-ticket funnel angle",
        "Conversion-focused positioning angle",
        "Clarity and execution angle",
    ]
    results = []
    for i in range(count):
        batch_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        batch_kwargs["variant"] = base_variants[i % len(base_variants)]
        results.append(build_product_pack(**batch_kwargs))
    return results

import uuid

from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "data" / "outputs"
OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def _make_pdf(title: str, content: str, filename: str) -> str:
    path = OUTPUT_DIR / filename
    c = canvas.Canvas(str(path), pagesize=letter)

    y = 750
    c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 16)
    c.drawString(50, y, title)

    y -= 40
    c.setFont("Helvetica", 11)

    for line in content.split("\n"):
        if y < 50:
            c.showPage()
            c.setFont("Helvetica", 11)
            y = 750
        c.drawString(50, y, line[:100])
        y -= 15

    c.save()
    return filename

import re
import uuid
from pathlib import Path
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "data" / "outputs"
OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def _slugify(value: str) -> str:
    value = re.sub(r"[^a-zA-Z0-9]+", "_", (value or "").strip()).strip("_").lower()
    return value or "product_pack"


def _recommended_price(promise: str, bonus: str) -> int:
    score = 9
    if (promise or "").strip():
        score += 10
    if (bonus or "").strip():
        score += 10
    return score


def _make_pdf(title: str, content: str, filename: str) -> str:
    path = OUTPUT_DIR / filename
    c = canvas.Canvas(str(path), pagesize=letter)

    y = 750
    c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 16)
    c.drawString(50, y, title)

    y -= 40
    c.setFont("Helvetica", 11)

    for line in content.split("\n"):
        if y < 50:
            c.showPage()
            c.setFont("Helvetica", 11)
            y = 750
        c.drawString(50, y, line[:100])
        y -= 15

    c.save()
    return filename


def build_product_pack(
    topic: str,
    niche: str,
    tone: str,
    buyer: str,
    promise: str = "",
    bonus: str = "",
    notes: str = "",
    base_url: str = "",
):
    topic_text = (topic or "Digital Product").strip()
    niche_text = (niche or "General").strip()
    tone_text = (tone or "Premium").strip()
    buyer_text = (buyer or "Beginners").strip()
    promise_text = (promise or f"Get faster results with {topic_text}").strip()
    bonus_text = (bonus or "Bonus checklist").strip()
    notes_text = (notes or "No additional notes.").strip()

    title = f"{topic_text.title()} Execution Pack"
    subtitle = f"A practical starter offer for {buyer_text} in {niche_text}"
    recommended_price = _recommended_price(promise_text, bonus)
    filename = f"{_slugify(topic_text)}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}.pdf"
    file_url = f"{base_url}/api/file/{filename}" if base_url else f"/api/file/{filename}"

    content = f"""
Topic: {topic_text}
Niche: {niche_text}
Tone: {tone_text}
Target Buyer: {buyer_text}

Positioning:
{subtitle}

Core Promise:
{promise_text}

Included Bonus:
{bonus_text}

Recommended Price:
${recommended_price}

Delivery Format:
PDF execution pack

Notes:
{notes_text}

Simple Offer Stack:
1. Front-end: {title}
2. Order bump: {bonus_text}
3. CTA: Download now and implement today

Steps to Execute:
1. Identify your audience
2. Create high-value content
3. Package into a sellable product
4. Distribute on platforms
5. Optimize and scale
"""

    _make_pdf(title, content, filename)

    return {
        "title": title,
        "subtitle": subtitle,
        "recommended_price": recommended_price,
        "bonus_included": bonus_text,
        "file_name": filename,
        "file_url": file_url,
    }


def build_product_batch(**kwargs):

    results = []
    for i in range(count):

    return results

def build_product_batch(**kwargs):
    count = kwargs.get("count", 3)
    base_topic = kwargs.get("topic", "Digital Product")
    results = []
    for i in range(count):
        batch_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        batch_kwargs["notes"] = (kwargs.get("notes", "") or "").strip()
        if batch_kwargs["notes"]:
            batch_kwargs["notes"] += f"\nVariant: Pack {i + 1}"
        else:
            batch_kwargs["notes"] = f"Variant: Pack {i + 1}"
        batch_kwargs["topic"] = f"{base_topic} Pack {i + 1}" if count > 1 else base_topic
        results.append(build_product_pack(**batch_kwargs))
    return results
